= AsciiDoc Cheat Sheet
:toc:
:showtitle:

A reference fixture exercising every AsciiDoc feature uatu's renderer supports.
Open this file in `bun run dev testdata/watch-docs` to see how each construct
looks; the same file is asserted against in unit and E2E tests.

== Headings

Six levels of nesting, mapping to `<h1>`–`<h6>`. The doctitle above is `<h1>`;
each `==` adds one heading level.

=== Level 3 heading

==== Level 4 heading

===== Level 5 heading

====== Level 6 heading (deepest)

== Inline Formatting

*bold*, _italic_, `monospace`, ~subscript~, ^superscript^, and a hard line +
break (note the trailing `+`).

A paragraph with regular flow, https://example.com[an external link], and an
inline cross-reference to <<source-listings>>.

== Lists

=== Unordered

* one
* two
** nested two-A
** nested two-B
*** deeper still
* three

=== Ordered

. first
. second
.. nested second-A
.. nested second-B
. third

=== Description

Term::
  Definition body.
Another term::
  Another definition. Description lists handle longer prose well too.

=== Checklist

* [x] completed task
* [ ] pending task

== Tables

|===
| Feature | Status | Notes

| GFM-equivalent body
| done
| Section, lists, tables, formatting

| `[source,LANG]` highlight
| done
| highlight.js token coloring

| `[source,mermaid]`
| done
| Hydrated client-side

| `include::` directive
| disabled
| SECURE safe mode (matches GitHub)
|===

[#source-listings]
== Source Listings

Highlighted JavaScript with two callouts:

[source,javascript]
----
function answer(input) {
  const value = input * 2; // <1>
  return value + 1;        // <2>
}
----
<1> Multiply input by two.
<2> Adjust by one before returning.

A listing whose language uatu's highlight registry doesn't know — it still
renders readably as plain code:

[source,fictional-lang]
----
when [thing] occurs, do other-thing.
----

== Mermaid Diagrams

`[source,mermaid]` listings hydrate into a real diagram (matches GitHub):

[source,mermaid]
----
graph TD
  Watch --> Render
  Render --> Sanitize
  Sanitize --> Browser
----

The bare `[mermaid]` block, by contrast, stays a literal block — also matching
GitHub, which only recognizes the `[source,mermaid]` form:

[mermaid]
....
this would NOT render as a diagram
....

== Admonitions

NOTE: Take note of this fact.

TIP: Try this trick to save time.

IMPORTANT: This setting affects every user.

CAUTION: Make sure to back up first.

WARNING: This action is irreversible.

== Quote and Sidebar Blocks

[quote, Albert Einstein, On Knowledge]
____
The only source of knowledge is experience.
____

[sidebar]
.A sidebar title
****
Sidebars are useful for tangential content that supports the main flow without
interrupting it. Asciidoctor wraps them in `<div class="sidebarblock">`.
****

== Footnotes

A claim that needs backing upfootnote:[Footnotes collect at the bottom of the
document automatically.] and another onefootnote:[Multiple footnotes accumulate
in order.] for good measure.

== Includes Are Disabled

Per Asciidoctor's SECURE safe mode (the same GitHub uses), `include::`
directives are not resolved. The line below renders as a clickable link to the
unresolved target rather than embedding any external content:

include::not-a-real-file.adoc[]
